title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index

The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This choropleth map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for 2020, using a continuous color scale to represent index scores ranging from 0 to 0.8. The legend defines the gradient, where the lightest shade corresponds to the lowest scores near 0 and the darkest navy blue indicates the highest scores approaching 0.8, while hatched areas denote regions with no available data. The highest values, represented by the darkest blue band, are visible across North America, Western Europe, Australia, New Zealand, and Japan. Canada, the United States, Norway, and Australia sit at the top of this distribution. A second tier of high performance, shown in medium-dark blue, covers much of South America, including Chile and Argentina, as well as parts of Eastern Europe and Russia. Mid-range scores, depicted in lighter blue shades, appear across Mexico, China, and Turkey. The lowest values on the map, indicated by the palest colors, are concentrated across sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia, including nations such as Niger, Chad, and Yemen. Greenland and a few other territories appear with hatching, indicating no data is reported for these areas. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of the highest index scores in the Global North and Oceania, while the lowest scores cluster in the Global South.
